title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at a sold-out concert at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan followed the album with tracks from Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark', highlighting his connection to Petty.
The encore featured two Wallflowers songs and two Tom Petty classics, delighting the Cabot audience.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts last year, losing 1,338 residents, bringing its population to 672,973.
Everett and Worcester experienced the biggest population gains in the state, with increases of 2,362 and 1,916 residents, respectively.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 in 2025, a 0.2% increase, slower than the previous year's nearly 1% growth rate.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments, led by the Johnson family, ranks 14th on Forbes list with $37.7 billion annual revenue.
Demoulas Super Markets, parent of Market Basket, ranks 69th with $8 billion in annual revenue, fully owned by Demoulas family.
Forbes list features US's largest multigenerational family businesses, spanning industries from retail to automotive manufacturing.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major housing revitalization project targeting affordability and modern amenities.
The redevelopment will occur in phases to ensure students remain housed while existing buildings are renovated or replaced.
The housing project aligns with state housing priorities, addressing high living costs and increasing student enrollment pressures.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford, showcasing songs from her protest-themed album 'World's Gone Wrong'.
The concert included covers of Bob Marley's 'So Much Trouble in the World' and Neil Young's 'Rockin’ in the Free World'.
Williams' performance highlighted her political commentary, addressing themes of protest and dissent with her new and old songs.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Justin Slaten gave up four runs, including two home runs, in two-thirds of an inning during Red Sox's 8-6 loss to the Twins.
Chad Tracy defended pulling Payton Tolle after 85 pitches, despite Tolle's nine strikeouts and solid performance post-second inning.
Boston Red Sox are down in the series against the Minnesota Twins after Friday's game at Fenway Park.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the first pitch at Fenway Park to his son, Twins player Kody Clemens.
The event coincided with the Boston Red Sox giving away Roger Clemens bobbleheads to fans at Fenway.
Kody Clemens, primarily a first baseman, is in his second season with the Minnesota Twins.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team with 58 votes for first time in career.
White recorded career-high 98 blocks, 88 steals, leading all guards with 552 contested shots and 1.3 blocks per game.
Celtics ended 2025-26 season 56-26, second in Eastern Conference, but exited first round against 76ers.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Glory', an Oscar-winning Civil War drama, is streaming on Netflix until June 1.
'Arco', a French animated film featuring a star-studded English dub cast, is available on Hulu.
'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed', starring Tatiana Maslany, is now streaming on Apple TV.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Garrett Crochet will have a throwing session this weekend and live batting practice next week for the Red Sox.
Roman Anthony is undergoing further evaluation but struggles with pain tolerance after a recent batting attempt.
Trevor Story is out for 6-10 weeks following sports hernia surgery, announced by manager Chad Tracy.
